items.title: The Dow Hits a Record Close
items.description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average got its first all-time high of 2026—but fell just short of closing above 49000 for the first time.  The index was up as much as 1.7% in midday trading, then faded a bit to end the day at 48977.18, up 1.2%.  The move to a new closing high came in part thanks to a jump in shares of Chevron, the sole remaining U.S. oil major in Venezuela.

items.title: Tuesday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
items.description: The Dow Industrials touched an all-time high and closed at a record Monday after the U.S. captured Venezuela's president over the weekend.

items.title: Goldman Leads Dow Jones As These Financial Firms Break To New Highs
items.description: Goldman Sachs stock jumped more than 4% Monday afternoon, making it the largest contributor to the Dow Jones Industrial Average's 1.6% afternoon gain.
